titleStmt.title: SII 38.212: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 212. (A.R. No. 212 of 1912.) TIRUVOṞṞIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On the north wall, first prākāra, Ādhipurīśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Rājanārāyaṇa Sambhuvarāya I Year 5: 1343 A.D.

summary.p: Records that in the presence of the pontiff of the Bhikshā maṭha of Perumpaṟṟappuliyūr, a meeting of the pontiff of Kīḻai-maṭham, Satyābharaṇa (a temple official), śrīmāhēśvaras, sthānattār, nāṭṭār, Vīrachōḻa-aṇukkar and kaikkōḷar took place at the hall called Vyākaraṇadāṉaṉ in Tiruvoṟṟiyūr to regulate the services of the dancing-girls padiyilār, dēvaraḍiyār and ishabattaḷiyilār. Accordingly they had allocated the duties to be discharged by the above dancing-girls and also the order of precedence to be given in the execution of duties such as singing, dancing, reciting hymns, fanning the god with fly-whisk and similar other duties in the temple. It is also recorded that the new regulations were warranted because the padiyilārs had been reduced to straiten condition since the days of Kottapparāyar alias Vāṇarāyar, a supervisory official of this village (Tiruvoṟṟiyūr) during the reign of Sundara Pāṇḍya and the present arrangement aimed at assisting the padiyilār by ishabattaḷiyilār in the discharge of duties. It was also declared that there be no discrimination in the discharge of duties by the padiyilār and dēvaraḍiyār and as per the old custom their remuneration was also fixed.
The imprecatory portion records that those who violate this arrangement would incur the sin of negating the royal command and betrayed the cause of lord Śiva.
Satyābaraṇadēvan and two others figure as signatories.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . Irājanārāyaṇaṉ campuvarāyaṟkku yāṇṭu Añcāvatu tiruvoṟṟiyūr vyākaraṇatāṉaṉ tirukkāvaṇattu taṉiyūr perumpaṟṟappuliyūrilThese two words are found engraved above the line. It is evident that engraver had forgotten to engrave these two words in the beginning but later on wrote them above.mutaliyār bhikṣāmaṭattu mutaliyār tirumuṉpāka kiḻaimaṭattumutaliyārum coḻamaṇṭalattil taṉimāhesvararil cattiyāparaṇarum Ittirukkāvaṇattu śrīmāheśvararum sthāṉattārum nāṭṭārum viracoḻaaṇukkarum kaik
koḷarum Iruntu patiyilār tevaraṭiyār koyilcevakattukku Ivarkaḷ porunta Aṟutiyiṭṭapaṭikku kalveṭṭiṉa muṉṉāḷ perumāḷ (perumāḷ) Emmaṇṭalamumkoṇṭa perumāḷ cuntarapāṇṭiyatevar nāḷil kottapparāyar Āṉa vāṇarāyar Ivvūrile Atikāramāka Irunta nāḷil nāṅkaḷ Aṉaivarum porunta niṟccayittappaṭi patiyilār cettum keṭṭu
m poka yiccurukkamākayil Ivarkaḷukku Utaviyāka Iṣapattaḷiyilārai pukutaviṭukaiyil tevaraṭiyār muṉpu ceytu ponta muṟaimai Āṉa tiruvalaku tirumeḻukkut taḷikai viḷakku Amutupaṭikku Arici kuṟṟaḷakkiṟa Ituvum tavirttu Itukku veṟe nimantamum kaṭṭip patiyilār cantikkuṉippam Iṭumpoḻutu tevaraṭiyār nācciyārkku canti
kkuṉippam Iṭavum nācciyārkku pakalolakkamillātirukkaThese two letters are engraved below the line.ItuThe letter tu is engraved below.vum kuṭuttu tiruttoppile Uṭaṉolakkamāka Irukkavum kuṭuttu veṇcāmaramum kavariyum paṇimāṟumiṭattu perumāḷukku mutaliyāṟkkum patiyilār muṉpum tevaraṭiyār Ivarkaḷ piṉpumāka paṇimāṟak kaṭavarkaḷākavum tevaraṭiyār tiruniṟṟukkāppum puṣpat taḷikaiyeṭukkumiṭaṅkaḷil Ellā viṭaṅkaḷumeṭukkavum Iṣapattaḷiyilā
rum Akamāṟkamum varikkolamum Āṭavum tiruttoppil tiruvolakkattukku varikkolamāṭakkaṭavarkaḷākavum patiyilār cokkamāṭum poḻutum cantik kuṉippamiṭumpoḻutum Iṣapattaḷiyilār pāṭakkaṭavarkaḷākavum patiyilār pāṭumiṭakkaḷilum cipalikkup pāṭumpoḻutu Iṣapattaḷiyilār pāṭavum tiruveṇcāmaram tiruvantikkāppum Iṣapattaḷiyilār Eṭukkakkaṭavarkaḷallāvākavum patiyi
lāru tevaraṭiyārum cevikkumiṭattu cevakattāḻvu paṭātapaṭi cevikka kaṭavarkaḷākavum patiyilār muṉpu Āṇṭu Oṉṟukku peṟum nellu muppatiṉkalamum peṟakkaṭavarkaḷākavum tevaraṭiyārkku per mutal paṭiye per Oṉṟukku nāḻi coṟu peṟakkaṭavatākavum Ippaṭikku cevakattāḻvupaṭāmal cevikkavum Inta tiṭṭuppaṭi Oḻiyac ceytār Uṇṭākil tampi
rāṉār Āṇaimaṟuttār paṭṭatuvum paṭṭu civatturokikaḷ paṭṭatuvum paṭṭu Iṉatturokikaḷ nāṭṭutturokikaḷ camaiyatturokikaḷ paṭṭatuvum paṭṭu Ippaṭikku Ituve sākṣipatramum Ituve toṭaiyum Ituve kovikkaccamum Ituve kaiolaiyum vāymuṭṭumāka koṉṟu kai muṟittukkoḷḷak kaṭavatākavum cammatittu Icaivu muṟi
tiṭṭu Iṭṭom patiyilārum viracoḻaaṇukkarum tevaraṭiyārum Ivvaṉaivarom lavirayam kaṇṭhedaśvadeśi Asyale virayam varagaśvara devasya § livirayattu varuṣa devasya § Ippaṭikku Ivai cattiyāparaṇatevar Eḻuttu § Ivai tevarkaḷ ṉāyiṉār Eḻuttu Ivai maṭauṭaiyār Eḻuttu .Below this another inscription runs as follows. 1 tirumācit tiruvāṉaṉ tirukkāvaṇattu kaikoḷarum itdha[r]mam vāgiśvarapaṇḍiten § ivai ⎯⎯m piṭārar svastiśrī sakāpdam āyirattu irunūṟṟu to[ṇṇu]ṟṟiṉ mel uṭaiyār kampaṇa uṭaiy[ā*]ṟku cellā niṉṟa ⎯⎯ 2 m peṟa aśvati nāḷ patiyilār tevar ⎯⎯ku camainta toṇṭaiyaṉ § veḷāṉ eḻuttu § ••••••perumpaṟṟapuliyūr malaimaṭattu mutaliyār keṭṭu niṟutti veṭṭiṉ kalveṭṭum poruntāmal piṉpu tāṉattār cattiya payiṟcil poruntāmal kalaintu kiṭakkaiyil āṉaikuntikkaramaracavi ø�
Digital edition of SII 38.212 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
